course_title: TVET Competence-Based Learning and Workplace Evidence
downloadable_title: TVET Quality Review and Improvement - Professional Downloadable Toolkit
core_terms: observable work tasks, workplace scenarios and practical demonstration evidence
intended_users: TVET trainers, assessors, workplace mentors and programme leaders
short_explanation: This downloadable toolkit supports TVET Quality Review and Improvement within TVET Competence-Based Learning and Workplace Evidence. It helps participants translate Finnish education principles into a reusable professional artefact, not simply complete a reading activity.
okm_framing: The resource connects classroom, leadership and institutional development to the wider Ministry of Education and Culture policy context: education improvement should be planned as part of system development, quality, inclusion, research-informed practice and public responsibility.
edufi_framing: The resource uses EDUFI-aligned wording by treating curriculum and learning design as a common foundation that supports equality while allowing local adaptation. Participants connect national-level principles to local curriculum, school culture, pedagogy and learner experience.
fineec_framing: The resource uses FINEEC-benchmarked wording by applying enhancement-led development: participation, trust, learning, appreciation, strengths, development targets and impact. Evidence is used to improve practice, not to blame individuals.

key_definitions.principle: the educational value or idea being made visible in practice
key_definitions.problem: the real learning, teaching, leadership, curriculum or institutional challenge being addressed
key_definitions.tool: the template, routine, planning document, dialogue structure or evidence instrument used to respond to the challenge
key_definitions.evidence: visible proof that the action has been applied, reviewed and connected to improvement
key_definitions.improvement_cycle: identify priority, plan action, implement, collect evidence, reflect, adjust and decide the next step
key_definitions.enhancement_led_development: a participatory, trust-based improvement approach focused on learning, strengths, development targets and impact

meaning_in_practice: In practice, participants use the toolkit to identify one priority related to TVET Quality Review and Improvement, choose a manageable action, complete the professional template, collect evidence and decide what should improve next.
evidence_requirement: observable work task, workplace scenario and practical demonstration evidence
model_example: A participant notices that tvet quality review and improvement is discussed in general terms but is not yet visible in daily practice. They complete the toolkit by selecting one priority, designing a focused action, collecting one piece of evidence, and writing a next-step improvement decision.
reflection_prompt: What changed in practice after using this TVET Quality Review and Improvement resource? What evidence supports your judgement? What should be continued, adapted, scaled or redesigned?
certificate_rule: The downloadable evidence is complete when the participant submits a filled TVET Quality Review and Improvement - Professional Downloadable Toolkit, includes evidence connected to observable work task, workplace scenario and practical demonstration evidence, and writes one improvement decision aligned to the FCEI Finnish Teacher Training Development Framework.
